name: Brenes from Seville - circular via San José de la Rinconada
name_original: Brenes desde Sevilla – circular por San José de la Rinconada
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around La Vega offer a diverse landscape primarily characterized by river corridors and greenways, providing accessible paths for various skill levels. The terrain features gentle elevations, with routes often following waterways and connecting urban areas with natural parks. This region provides opportunities for exploring both historical sites and natural reserves via dedicated cycling infrastructure. Best touring cycling routes around La Vega The most popular touring cycling route is City tour of Seville - circular route, a 20.0 miles (32.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 57 minutes to complete. This route offers a comprehensive tour through urban and historical landscapes. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Anfiteatro de Italica in Santiponce from Sevilla – circular via the Via Verde de Italica, an easy 15.9 miles (25.7 km) path. This route follows a greenway, leading to historical sites. Local touring cyclists also love the Puerta Verde de Alcalá de Guadaíra Trail, a 15.0 miles (24.2 km) trail leading through green corridors, often completed in about 1 hour 44 minutes. Touring cycling around La Vega is defined by river paths, green corridors, and urban-to-natural park connections. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy city loops to moderate trails with varied terrain. The routes in La Vega are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 800 reviews. More than 10,000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore La Vega's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around La Vega?
A: La Vega offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with over 550 routes available. These range from easy river paths to more challenging mountain trails, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in La Vega?
A: The terrain around La Vega is diverse, primarily featuring river corridors and greenways with gentle elevations. However, as a gateway to the Central Mountain Range, you can also find routes with varied elevations and scenic mountain passes, offering both accessible paths and more challenging climbs.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in La Vega?
A: The touring cycling routes in La Vega are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 800 reviews. Cyclists often praise the lush green landscapes, the refreshing mountain weather, and the variety of routes that connect urban areas with natural parks.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in La Vega?
A: Yes, La Vega has numerous easy touring cycling routes, with over 170 options available. An excellent choice for beginners is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991702884/alcala-del-rio-from-seville-circular-via-la-algaba-and-la-rinconada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcala del Rio from Seville – circular via La Algaba and La Rinconada&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a gentle ride through pleasant scenery.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in La Vega?
A: Absolutely. Many touring cycling routes in La Vega are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988481624/rio-pudio-green-corridor-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Pudio Green Corridor Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate option that takes you through a scenic green corridor.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that pass by waterfalls or natural pools?
A: While specific routes in our guide may not directly list waterfalls, the La Vega region is renowned for its impressive cascades like Aguas Blancas (the highest in the Caribbean), Cascada del Rio Blanco, and Cascada de Jimenoa. Natural pools like Pozo Azul also offer picturesque stops. Many routes traverse the lush, mountainous terrain where such natural features are common, providing opportunities for exploration.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions I can see along the touring cycling routes?
A: The routes around La Vega offer a blend of natural beauty and cultural sights. You can explore historical sites and natural reserves. Notable attractions in the wider area include the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/215404" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Seville Cathedral&lt;/a&gt;, the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/112737" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza of Spain&lt;/a&gt;, and the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5343886" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tower of Gold&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, you might encounter beautiful lakes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2676623" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Huéznar Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2676625" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lago Azul&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in La Vega?
A: La Vega benefits from cooler mountain weather compared to coastal areas. While it's a tropical climate, the mountain influence provides a generally pleasant temperature range of 24–29°C (75–84°F) for much of the year, making it suitable for touring cycling throughout most seasons.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that are suitable for families?
A: Many of the easy and moderate routes, especially those following river paths and green corridors, are suitable for families. These paths often feature gentle gradients and provide a safe environment for cyclists of all ages. Consider routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990407001/la-corchuela-park-and-guadaira-river-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Corchuela Park and Guadaíra River Loop&lt;/a&gt; for a family-friendly experience.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that are dog-friendly?
A: While specific dog-friendly designations are not always explicit, many of the natural park trails and greenways are generally suitable for cycling with dogs, provided they are kept on a leash and you adhere to local regulations. It's always best to check specific park rules before heading out. Routes through open natural areas tend to be more accommodating.

Q: Where can I find parking for touring cycling routes in La Vega?
A: Parking availability varies by route. For routes that start in or near urban centers, public parking lots or street parking may be available. For routes venturing into natural parks or more rural areas, designated trailheads often provide parking facilities. It's advisable to check the starting point of your chosen route for specific parking information.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that connect to public transport?
A: Many routes around La Vega, especially those starting from larger towns or cities, can be accessed via public transport. While specific connections aren't detailed for every route, urban-to-natural park connections are a feature of the region's cycling network, making it possible to combine cycling with public transit for part of your journey.
tips:
The Walls of Seville (Spanish: Murallas de Sevilla) are a series of defensive walls that surround the old town of Seville.
The city has been surrounded by walls since Roman times, which were preserved and modified during the subsequent Visigothic, Islamic and finally Castilian periods. The walls remained intact until the 19th century, when they were partially demolished after the Revolution of 1868.

The Puerta de la Macarena, also known as the Arco de la Macarena, is one of the three remaining city gates of the old walls of Seville. It is located on Calle Resolana in the barrio de San Gil, opposite the Basílica de la Macarena. The gate was originally built in the 12th century during the rule of the Almoravids and later expanded and rebuilt in the 18th century.
